title: revert-d6c366d-or-grace-window-stop-churn
description:
ROLLBACK DECISION. Live production regression committed by d6c366d (PR #32 bead u3w). Symptom: ezgha daemon is in a 'respawn → register → reap → respawn' loop at ~3 cycles/min. 15 respawns in 5 minutes on a 16-slot fleet means an entire slot is being chomped every ~4 minutes. Root cause: release_stale_slots Path 4 (the new offline_not_busy_owned_missing_container_registrations helper) reaps a GH runner when prefix_match ∧ offline ∧ !busy ∧ !local_container_names.contains — but for the ~5s window between ensure_count respawn and GitHub jitconfig propagation back, the local_container_names tracking has not been updated yet, so the predicate matches and Path 4 calls github::remove_runner on the brand-new id. CodeRabbit dedup fix 8368b7e prevents double-reap inside one tick but the HashSet is local to one tick — multiple ticks race across the boundary. Production data: c-15 respawned 4x in 5min, slot_assignments.toml shows slots churning ids constantly. Synthesis §6 explicitly warned no grace window in v1 and that proved wrong in production.

DECISION TREE for the deploy-owner (user):
(A) REVERT d6c366d (4th sub-pass Path 4 lines 370-422 + the helper fn that goes with them). LOSES: Mac zombie defense reverts to 'wait for server-side aging' as before 09:00 PDT. KEEPS: 422-class qbl fix + adafa19 hardening both live.
(B) STOP THE DAEMON NOW to break the loop, losing all 16 Linux slots until a fix lands.
(C) FAST-PATH GRACE WINDOW: have a coder write the registered-at grace window to slot_assignments.toml and release_stale_slots' Path 4 honoring it; deploy as a hotfix (ETA ~10 min). Preserves the s9d fix but stops the loop.

Plus followup beads to file:
- grace-window fix in 1-day followup
- qbl test for grace-window interaction (Path 2 cancel-then-delete also has same race against Path 1)
- stuck-runn  ers dashboard panel refresh.
notes: AUDIT UPDATE (2026-07-08 22:28): 4-track independent review + adversarial skeptic complete. REVISED root-cause picture: (1) watchdog-restart mechanism CONFIRMED — 15/20 systemd restarts over 3h match ezgha-fleet-watchdog.sh 'BELOW TARGET' lines to the second; timer was re-enabled 11:30:54 after being deliberately stopped (bead 2ik predicted this); daemon has zero SIGTERM handling so each restart can orphan in-flight registrations. (2) BUT skeptic volume-analysis shows restart-orphans explain only a MINORITY of churn — 92% of reclaims happen restart-free, so Path 4 grace-window (5ki) is the dominant fix. RECOMMENDED SEQUENCE: stop ezgha-watchdog.timer now (cheap, safe, kills the restart storms), ship 5ki grace-window (kills the volume), then decide on d6c366d revert only if churn persists after both. ALSO: PR #32 merged with ZERO completed reviews (fix-commit to merge = 5 seconds) — process gate failure confirmed by two independent forensic passes, no innocent explanation found.
close_reason: Decision (C) fast-path grace window TAKEN, per this bead's own decision tree: a coder wrote the registered-at grace window to slot_assignments.toml and release_stale_slots' Path 4 (+ Path 1) now honors it -- commit d0e814e. Preserves the s9d fix, stops the loop (pending deploy). NOT (A) full revert of d6c366d -- would have lost the Mac zombie defense and the qbl 422-class fix; NOT (B) stop-the-daemon -- confirmed no safety emergency (real jobs executing throughout, load nominal). This decision was already effectively validated by the review doc's own §7 remediation narrative (PR #33-#37 took the same fast-path-not-revert direction earlier); this session's live re-measurement confirmed churn was STILL happening post-#37 at a comparable rate to the original finding, so the grace-window fix genuinely needed to land (it wasn't a stale bead about an already-fixed problem). Followup beads from this bead's list: grace-window fix -> 5ki/lyf (closed), qbl-Path2-vs-Path1 timing test -> folded into existing coverage per 5ki closure note, stuck-runners dashboard panel -> not actioned, out of this mission's scope, left for a future session if wanted.

title: hostname-scoped deregistration residual collision + 'host' fallback

title: Process guardrail: prevent reactive-cascade / house-of-cards fix accretion
description:
2026-07-07/08 session: agent responded to a runner-fleet incident with a cascade of reactive point-fixes -- deployed po2 respawn-pacing gate to stop watchdog reboots, then relaxed the watchdog (removing po2's reason to exist), then po2's stale hardcoded ceiling (validation rejects >=24.0, tied to the OLD watchdog max-load-1=24) throttled fleet recovery even after the watchdog was raised to 96. The gap was correctly identified and captured as bead ez-gh-actions-rfk but filed as P2 "tune later" instead of treated as an urgent same-breath follow-up, so the fleet ran needlessly throttled. Net effect: a daemon that originally had zero respawn throttles accreted ~69 lines of interacting load-gate/ceiling/batch/budget logic in one day, and reactive fixes made the system more fragile, not less. Also involved repeated mis-diagnosis under pressure (declared po2 deployed-and-working while fleet was actually draining; blamed po2, then found GH rate limit; blamed budget, then found secondary rate limit) and premature success declarations without ground-truth verification.

Tracking bead for applying the harness fixes recommended by the /harness meta-analysis (see conversation for full 5-Whys + proposed rule text): a "second-fix-means-stop-and-reassess" rule, an "audit and delete obsolete workarounds when you fix a root cause" rule, a "verify with ground truth before declaring success" rule, and a complexity-budget self-check for incident-pressure changes. Proposed homes: ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md (global agent-behavior rule, not repo-specific), a Claude memory entry, and a self-invoked checklist for 3rd+ consecutive fix to the same subsystem.

title: Daemon graceful shutdown (SIGTERM handling) — required before watchdog timer re-enablement
description:
GATING CONDITION for re-enabling ezgha-watchdog.timer (currently stopped, bead 2ik ships the
durable watchdog script but does NOT fix this). Per /advice Reviewer A (PR#34 review,
2026-07-08/09): even with the watchdog's new guardrails (3-consecutive-miss, load gate, 30-min
cooldown), a LEGITIMATE watchdog-triggered restart still orphans in-flight GitHub runner
registrations, because the ezgha daemon has ZERO SIGTERM handling today — any restart (watchdog,
manual, deploy) kills it mid-flight with no graceful drain.

REQUIRED FIX before the watchdog timer is re-enabled: implement a SIGTERM handler in the ezgha
daemon's serve loop that: (1) stops accepting/starting NEW runner registrations, (2) allows any
in-flight start_one() (JIT registration → docker run) to finish (or cleanly abort + release the
slot if it can't finish within a bounded grace period, e.g. 10-15s), (3) does NOT kill already-
running/registered runner containers (those should survive a daemon restart since ezgha reconciles
state from GitHub + docker on next start), (4) exits cleanly once safe.

Complements bead ez-gh-actions-7og (slot-lifecycle state machine consolidation) — a systemd/
daemon lifecycle contract is prerequisite context for that broader consolidation, not a
duplicate of it.

Source: /advice panel Reviewer A (opus), PR #34 review, relayed via sidekick-churn.

title: doctor.sh sections 7/8 can hang indefinitely under sustained Actions-API rate limit (undocumented bucket, not shown by gh api rate_limit)

comments.author: jleechan
comments.text: Discovered live during ez-gh-actions-g3o verification (2026-07-08): repos/{repo}/actions/runs/{id}/jobs returned {"message":"API rate limit exceeded for user ID ...","status":"403"} for 10+ minutes straight, while gh api rate_limit core bucket showed remaining=3569/5000 (not exhausted). This is a separate, undocumented Actions-API-specific throttle not exposed by any resource in the /rate_limit response. doctor.sh sections 7 (real job-execution proof) and 8 (queue-health.sh) both call this endpoint in a loop; under set -euo pipefail + process substitution, the script silently produces no further output for the remainder of the run once this bucket engages (observed: 3 separate full runs each stopped exactly at the section 7 header with 90s/300s/600s timeouts). Fix ideas: (1) wrap each per-run gh api call with a short timeout + explicit 403-rate-limit detection that skips remaining runs in the loop rather than hanging/silently truncating; (2) surface 'rate limited, N/6 runs checked' instead of going silent. Root-caused as external/orthogonal to the g3o fix -- section 9 (this session new per-slot proof) is immune since it is LOCAL-ONLY (docker top, no gh api calls).

title: fix(src/main.rs): wait_for_backend in-process retry around choose_backend
description: Currently src/main.rs:201-220 (Commands::Serve arm) calls choose_backend(cfg)?; — if it returns Err (e.g. Selection::None because lima isn't up yet), main() exits code 1, systemd restarts at RestartSec=10. The Jul 6 incident produced 1381 of these. Replace the immediate ? with wait_for_backend(cfg)?; which loops with capped exponential backoff (15s, 30s, 60s, 120s, 240s, 300s-cap) and only returns Ok once choose_backend succeeds. Once the daemon is in its main 30s loop, transient docker-socket blips are already contained; this change specifically protects the cold-start path. Concrete Rust patch in advisor review Q7. Verify with cargo test, then rebuild + restart per Gate 0.
notes: Implemented a9a7ab1: wait_for_backend() retries backend::select() for up to 120s, sleeping 5s between attempts. serve subcommand uses this instead of choose_backend().

title: REST+GraphQL bucket split for gh API calls -- fix registration starvation
description:
ROOT CAUSE (confirmed via journal 2026-07-07 22:47-22:51): github.rs routes ALL
calls through `gh api` (REST). REST and GraphQL are separate 5000/hr rate-limit
buckets. Heavy read-side monitoring (queue_monitor's queued-run enumeration,
per-run job listing, invariant_sampler's runner/job polling) exhausts the REST
bucket, starving the critical write path (generate_jitconfig / runner
registration, also REST) -- ephemeral runners can't register, exit immediately,
fleet bleeds toward 0 even though load/po2 are fine.

FIX:
1. Move READ-HEAVY monitoring to GraphQL: queue_monitor's queued-run + per-run
   job enumeration, and runner status listing. One GraphQL query replaces many
   REST calls -- more efficient AND a separate quota bucket.
2. KEEP registration/mutations on REST: generate_jitconfig has no GraphQL
   equivalent, must stay REST.
3. Add bucket-aware fallback: on a 403 in one transport, call `gh api
   rate_limit` (returns both buckets' remaining) and route to whichever bucket
   has budget; log which bucket is exhausted.

PROCESS (per user directive 2026-07-07): implement via codex (zero Claude
quota, Claude 429 pressure), small Claude swarm (2-3 agents max) for
adversarial review only, not a big fan-out. Branch: sidekick/rest-graphql-split.
TDD. Commit+push after every green unit of work, never hold >30min
uncommitted. Do NOT merge/deploy without main's review + a low-load window.

title: ensure-grace-window-on-online-registrations
description: Production followup: release_stale_slots Path 4 (offline+!busy self-heal, src/docker_backend.rs:370-422 at d6c366d) needs a registered-recently grace window. Track each successful registration's registered_at timestamp in slot_assignments.toml with a 60s grace window; release_stale_slots skips any registration whose registered_at is within the window. Without the grace window, ensure_count respawn (5s jitconfig propagation) races release_stale_slots (every 30s tick) and Path 4 reaps the freshly-registered id. Same race likely exists for qbl Path 2 (cancel-then-delete) racing Path 1 (slot release) since both run in the same tick. Tests: 5 new tests: (1) ensure_count respawn -> release_stale_slots within window does NOT reap; (2) after window elapses, release_stale_slots reclaims if conditions still met; (3) Path 2 cancel-then-delete timing vs Path 1; (4) cross-tick racing-registration same id; (5) the adafa19 + d6c366d regression test that would have caught this.
notes: SKEPTIC RE-PRIORITIZATION (2026-07-08 22:28 audit): this grace-window fix is the DOMINANT fix by churn volume, not a hardening nice-to-have. Independent skeptic re-analysis of a restart-free daemon window (PID 3356340, 21:50-21:54) found 92% of stale-slot reclaims and ~88% of respawns occur with NO restart nearby — Path 4 fires heavily during uninterrupted operation, so fixing the watchdog alone will NOT stop the churn. Ship this BEFORE or WITH the watchdog fix. Note: within-process mechanism for restart-free reclaims still needs instrumentation (premature reap vs legit cleanup of self-deregistration-lagged completions) — the grace window fixes both interpretations. Evidence: /tmp/ez-gh-actions/sidekick/runner-24h-review/tracks/skeptic.md
close_reason: FIXED: commit d0e814e on origin/main. Live re-measurement 2026-07-09 ~18:30 PDT confirmed g3i's finding still reproduced (306 respawns + 177 reclaims / 3h restart-free window on jeff-ubuntu 7b69853, single slots cycling 5-6x/15min, doctor-runner independently caught c-12 DOWN mid-run). Implemented the registered_at grace-window exactly per this bead's spec: BTreeMap<String,u64> on SlotAssignments, stamped by record_slot_runner_id_for, 60s window gates Path 1's offline+!busy branch AND Path 4 (offline_not_busy_owned_missing_container_registrations, now takes the assignments snapshot so it still sees a timestamp for a slot Path 1 evicted same-tick). 5 tests added matching this bead's exact 5-test list (minus the Path-2-vs-Path-1 timing test, folded into existing qbl-path2 coverage since that interaction was already independently tested). cargo test 220 passed, cargo clippy clean. Adversarial skeptic sub-agent independently verified: read the diff, re-ran tests/clippy itself, confirmed boundary math/slot-matching/fail-open-on-missing-entry are all correct, confirmed the root-cause ordering claim (record_slot_runner_id_for happens before docker run) by reading start_one directly. Skeptic found ONE real gap (forward-sweep orphan reaper + empty-id branch remain unprotected, narrower crash-window race, not the live-tick race this bead measured) -- filed as follow-up jleechan-64tl, does not block this closure. NOT yet deployed (no cargo install/restart run) -- deploy is main session's call per single-writer rule.

title: doctor-runner DOWN persistence window (25-30s) is shorter than a real respawn cycle under load — busy fleet still measures as dead
description: Evidence 2026-07-09 21:35-21:45, queue hot (5-17 queued, 21-24 in-progress): three consecutive doctor-runner runs each flagged a DIFFERENT slot persisted-DOWN (c-3, then c-7, then Mac b-2/b-5), and each flagged slot was observed 'Up <60s' immediately after — the daemon self-healed every one. Live docker ps at 21:44 showed c-9 absent and c-7 'Up Less than a second': under load, 1-2 slots are ALWAYS legitimately inside their respawn window (job finish -> deregister -> JIT token -> docker run takes 30-60s+, serialized through the 20s serve tick). The b895 2-sample persistence (25-30s apart) is SHORTER than that cycle, so a busy fleet still measures as dead — the exact defect class doctor-runner was built to eliminate. ALSO marginal: heartbeat gap measured 99s vs threshold 100s (5x20s tick) in the same run — the tick stretches under load; threshold will flap. FIX DIRECTION (one mechanism, not two): a slot should count DOWN only if containerless for longer than a full respawn cycle (e.g. 3+ samples spanning >=2-3 min, or cross-check the daemon journal for a respawn of that slot inside the window = CYCLING). Heartbeat threshold should tolerate observed busy-tick stretch (e.g. 8-10x tick or p99-based). NOTE: 4 doctor-runner truthfulness fixes shipped 2026-07-09 (b895, nj2j, wxfl/udzf, n9py/zlsz) — this is the 5th in the same theme; per complexity-budget rule consider whether section-9 local proof and section-10 inventory should be ONE code path before adding more per-mechanism fixes (they disagreed tonight: totals said 0 down while gates said 1-3 down).
close_reason: Fixed by 06e4299 (journal-crosscheck DOWN classification, 8x heartbeat threshold, section 9/10 consolidation); mirror bead closed by fix agent; verified live — first exit-0 doctor-runner runs 2026-07-10.

title: fix(lima-vm@colima.service): Type=oneshot + RemainAfterExit=yes + drop --foreground + TimeoutSec=300
description: Current unit at ~/.config/systemd/user/lima-vm@colima.service has Type=simple + TimeoutSec=10 and runs 'limactl start colima --foreground' in the foreground. On warm boot (VM already running) this completes in 16ms and seems fine; on cold boot, limactl needs minutes to bring up the QEMU guest, gets SIGKILLed by TimeoutSec=10, and the unit fails. Verified empirically with 'limactl start colima' on running VM (16ms 'Using the existing instance') and watching the cold-boot get killed. Fix unit to: drop --foreground (limactl daemonizes itself, returns when VM is SSH-able), Type=oneshot + RemainAfterExit=yes (so unit is 'active' after limactl returns, until asked to stop), TimeoutSec=10 → 300 (bound worst-case cold-boot cloud-init). Type=notify is NOT viable because limactl doesn't emit sd_notify. This single change is the highest leverage in the fix set; without it, no consumer-side Requires= will work on cold-boot.
notes: Implemented f202638: lima-vm@colima.service patched to Type=forking, drop --foreground, RemainAfterExit=yes, TimeoutStartSec=300, Restart=no, ExecStop=. Deployed + checked in to docs/lima-vm-colima.service.

title: Design periodic GitHub Actions stuck-run reaper
notes: Design target: add a periodic, dry-run-first stuck-run reaper for jleechanorg/worldarchitect.ai and other QUEUE_REPO values. It must be job-aware: do not classify a run as stale only by workflow_run.created_at because reruns/current attempts can have old run.created_at but new job.started_at/run_started_at. Proposed policy: every 10-15 minutes, scan queued and in_progress runs with REST; fetch jobs for candidates; compute current_attempt_age from max(job.started_at, run.run_started_at, run.updated_at) for queued jobs. Actions: (1) cancel-then-delete only queued run objects whose run.created_at > 8h AND current_attempt_age > 60-90m, (2) cancel superseded queued runs only when a newer queued or in_progress run exists for same repo/workflow/branch and candidate current_attempt_age > 20-30m, (3) never touch in_progress by default; report in_progress jobs over policy separately with runner/job URL, (4) label-mismatch detector reports queued jobs whose labels have no matching online runner labels, e.g. agent-orchestrator or warp-*; it does not cancel them. Install path: systemd user timer on Linux host using ~/.cargo/bin/ezgha or scripts/reap-stuck-runs.sh, with log file under ~/.local/state/ezgha/reaper.jsonl and alert() on mutations/failures. Safety: dry-run default, --apply only for timer after config enables it, max mutations per run, REST rate-limit floor, lock file, exact URLs in logs, no tail cancellation without explicit allowlist.

title: Consolidate slot-lifecycle state transitions into one owned module with explicit state machine + regression tests
description:
CROSS-INCIDENT ESCALATION per CLAUDE.md rule: "If 2+ incidents share a pattern in the last 2
weeks, escalate to a systemic fix instead of another targeted fix." 7 P0/P1 beads hit the same
slot-lifecycle/release_stale_slots subsystem in ~24h (2026-07-08/09): u3w (add Path 4),
0jo (revert-or-grace-window decision), lyf (Path 4 race with ensure_count), 5ki (grace window
fix), g3i (independent audit confirming the above + PR#32 process bypass), 2ik (watchdog restart
churn interacting with the same subsystem), and PR #33 / B1+B2 (Path 1 truncated-list mass-reclaim
+ fail-open None-container-list bug). Every one of these is a distinct edge case bolted onto the
same ~600-line release_stale_slots()/start_one_with_generate() region in src/docker_backend.rs,
with each fix (Path 1 / Path 2 qbl / Path 3 orphan sweep / Path 4 u3w) implemented as a separate
ad-hoc function with its own predicate logic, its own warning strings, and its own test file
section — no single source of truth for "what state is this slot/runner-id pair actually in, and
what transitions are legal."

PROPOSAL (design-only until scoped by an owner — do NOT start implementation preemptively per the
complexity-budget checkpoint; this bead exists to hold the systemic-fix commitment, not to trigger
immediate work):
- Define an explicit enum for slot lifecycle state (e.g. Empty, Reserved, Registering,
  RegisteredNoContainer, RegisteredWithContainer, OfflineIdleNoContainer, OfflineBusyNoContainer
  (422-zombie), StaleUnknown (docker ps failed / API truncated)) and the legal transitions between
  them.
- Move Path 1 (release_stale_slots_from_with_containers_for), Path 2 (qbl cancel-then-delete),
  Path 3 (orphan forward sweep), and Path 4 (offline_not_busy_owned_missing_container_registrations)
  into one module that computes the CURRENT state per slot once per tick, then dispatches to exactly
  one handler per state — replacing today's pattern of 4 independent predicate functions each
  re-deriving overlapping conditions (offline? busy? local container present? live in GH list?)
  from the same raw inputs.
- Add regression tests keyed by STATE, not by historical bug number, so a future incident in this
  subsystem adds a test to an existing state's table instead of a 5th sibling function.
- Explicitly test the "unknown/degraded" case (docker ps failed, GitHub API truncated/unreachable)
  as ITS OWN first-class state (StaleUnknown) with a fail-safe (keep, don't reclaim) transition
  rule, rather than each Path independently getting this right or wrong (B2 in PR#33 was exactly
  this bug recurring for the 2nd+ time in the same subsystem).

Absorbs: g3i (closing g3i as absorbed into this bead — see g3i for the full audit trail this
escalation is based on).

title: Blast-radius review gate for threshold/health/monitor/restart changes (self-outage class)
description: Failure class: a safety/health/monitoring mechanism caused the very outage it was meant to prevent because a config/threshold change's blast radius and interactions were never evaluated. Real 2026-07-07 instances: (1) /etc/watchdog.conf max-load-1=24 on 32-thread box rebooted the host twice during normal Gate-0 mass respawns (~71 1-min load); (2) E1 invariant sampler drained the fleet to zero by starving the single-threaded serve loop; (3) slack_webhook_url unset + primary webhook 404 -> alerting silently dead all mission; (4) careful-restart rule lived in one session's context only. Deliverable = durable guardrails: (a) CLAUDE.md 'blast-radius / interaction review' rule for ANY change to a threshold/health-check/watchdog/restart-policy/resource-limit/monitor-cadence, requiring the change to state the normal peak of the bounded metric and the remaining margin; (b) principle 'a safety/health/monitoring mechanism must not be able to cause the failure it guards against'; (c) doctor.sh gate flagging watchdog max-load-1 vs observed respawn peak margin; (d) version-control /etc/watchdog.conf + margin rationale in-repo. Related: ez-gh-actions-po2 (daemon respawn pacing), ez-gh-actions-2ik (commit external watchdog script), queue_monitor::SERVE_LOOP_TIME_BUDGET (serve-loop starvation test).

If RUNNER_NAME_PREFIX or MAC_RUNNER_NAME_PREFIX (config-driven, operator-settable) ever contains a regex metacharacter (., *, [, ^, $, etc.), the pattern could match unintended log lines (false CYCLING classification, masking a real DOWN) or fail to match the intended one (false DOWN). title: graceful daemon re-exec (ezgha reload) — zero-respawn-wave code rollouts
description:
User's idea (2026-07-07, via main during po2 deploy). Currently every ezgha
daemon code update requires systemctl --user restart ezgha.service, which
kills and respawns the daemon process -- runner containers survive (separate
Docker procs) but the daemon re-adopts them cold via ensure_count on the new
process's first tick, and if timed wrong (load>12, containers<12) this has
tripped the host watchdog reboot twice in one day (2026-07-07).

Proposed: an `ezgha reload` command/signal that does an in-place exec() of
the new binary between serve-loop ticks (like nginx/systemd graceful reload),
re-adopting the already-running containers via the existing slot_assignments
+ ensure_count reconciliation path rather than a full process kill+restart.
Combined with the po2 gate-primary respawn pacing (merged 2026-07-07,
commit d612ad7/deployed 6a3138d), this would eliminate the restart-driven
reboot risk entirely -- code rollouts would never need the careful-restart
load<12/containers>=12 gate because there's no respawn wave to pace, just a
process re-exec with the existing container fleet immediately re-adopted.

Do NOT implement yet -- P2, capture only. Implementation routes through
/sidekick (sidekick3) or a swarm when picked up, not direct main coding,
per the 2026-07-07 delegation directive.

description: THE real P0 per advice-p0 root-cause analysis. Today's mechanism of harm: N concurrent agent sessions across 2 machines each build+cargo-install+restart ezgha racing each other, with NO serialization. title: Watchdog churn-guard masks a genuine stuck-serve-loop stall — pre-arm bead before timer re-enablement
description:
CONFIRMED DESIGN GAP from /advice Reviewer C cold-review pass (PR #34, 2026-07-09), architectural
reasoning to be verified in code as part of this bead's work:

The watchdog's "ephemeral churn, don't restart" guard (actual managed-container count below
target BUT reserved-slot count >= target -> assume the daemon is just mid-respawn-cycle, skip
restart, do not even count it as a miss) is correct for the common case but MASKS the genuine
"daemon's single-threaded serve loop is fully stuck" scenario: release_stale_slots runs inside
that same serve loop, so a truly stuck loop means no reaping happens, the slot file stays frozen
at its last-known state (typically == configured count, satisfying the churn guard's condition),
and the guard therefore reads as permanently true — the watchdog would never fire, believing
"ephemeral churn" indefinitely while the daemon is actually dead in the water.

Why tonight's original (pre-2ik) watchdog DID trip during the real incident: the daemon was ALIVE
and actively reaping/respawning (slots dropping below configured, not frozen at it) — this is a
DIFFERENT failure mode than a genuine full stall, and the pre-arm gap only applies to the stall
case, not the actively-churning case this PR's guardrails already handle well.

REQUIRED before the watchdog timer is ever re-enabled (in addition to the reboot-stale-state
guard bead ez-gh-actions-xfw and the SIGTERM/graceful-shutdown bead ez-gh-actions-30p /
ez-gh-actions-uh2): the churn guard needs an independent STALENESS SIGNAL that can distinguish
"slots frozen because healthy churn just settled" from "slots frozen because the loop is dead" --
e.g. journal/log freshness (has the daemon logged ANY reconciliation-tick activity in the last N
minutes?) or a "last successful respawn timestamp" the daemon itself writes and the watchdog can
read independently of the slot file. Verify the architectural reasoning above against the actual
serve-loop code (src/main.rs or wherever the reconciliation loop lives) as the first step of this
bead's work -- confirm release_stale_slots really does run inside the single-threaded loop with
no independent liveness heartbeat before designing the fix.

LOWEST PRIORITY, note only (Reviewer C#7/8, do not action unless trivial when this bead is
worked): (a) exit-code aggregation across check_mac/check_linux could be clearer than the current
EXIT=$? accumulation pattern; (b) watchdog.log has no rotation/size cap, could grow unbounded over
long uptimes.

Source: /advice Reviewer C (cold review), PR #34, relayed via sidekick-churn.

title: release-stale-slots-races-respawn-grace-window-needed
description:
CRITICAL REGRESSION in PRODUCTION. The new u3w 4th sub-pass (commit d6c366d, src/docker_backend.rs release_stale_slots + the new offline_not_busy_owned_missing_container_registrations helper) is racing with ensure_count in a churn loop: daemon respawns slot 15 (~5s to register via jitconfig) → release_stale_slots runs in that ~5s window → sees the freshly-registered id as offline-not-busy-not-in-local-containers list → calls github::remove_runner on the brand-new registration → next ensure_count tick respawns again → loop. Live evidence: 17 respawns in 5 min, slot ez-runner-c-15 respawned 4 times in 5 min, slot_assignments.toml shows slot 15 at runner-id 141563 but GitHub registry no longer has it (or is replacing it constantly). CodeRabbit's dedup fix 8368b7e only prevents double-reaping inside ONE tick — the dedup HashSet is local, ticks race across the boundary.

Root-cause class: the synthesis §6 recommendation was NO grace window in v1, and that proved wrong in production — there is no grace window for JIT registration propagation. The qbl 422-zombie path (lines 406-410) has the same potential race against Path 1.

FIX SKELETON:
- Add a registered-recently grace window (e.g. 60s) tracked in slot_assignments.toml with a registered_at timestamp per id; skip any registration whose registered_at is within the window.
- OR: ensure_count should mark each respawn's runner_id as 'fresh, skip-reap' for the next 60s, and release_stale_slots should consult that flag.
- Tests: 5 new tests covering: (1) ensure_count respawns → release_stale_slots within window does NOT reap; (2) after window, release_stale_slots reclaims; (3) graceful missed reaping; (4) qbl-cancel-then-delete timing vs Path 1; (5) cross-tick racing runner.

DEPLOY BLOCKER: until fixed, every restart of the daemon triggers this loop, and the fleet cannot hold 22/22 because slot 15 (and probably others) is being chomped every cycle. ALSO: the auto-factory's 4kv deploy plan would RE-INTRODUCE this race on every restart cycle even on a clean binary. Recommend rolling back d6c366d + 8368b7e to a known-good state (adafa19+qbl-fix on origin/main pre d6c366d, which DID NOT have this race because Path 4 didn't exist yet) before further restarts.

P0 - root-cause bug, production regression
close_reason: Duplicate/companion of ez-gh-actions-5ki, same fix. Closed together: commit d0e814e implements the registered_at grace window this bead's FIX SKELETON described (option 1: registered_at timestamp per id, grace window in release_stale_slots). See 5ki's closure note for full evidence chain (live re-measurement, implementation detail, test coverage, skeptic verification). This bead's DEPLOY BLOCKER note is resolved by the fix landing in source; actual fleet deployment is a separate main-session action per single-writer rule, tracked via the standing Gate 0 deploy checklist, not by this bead.

title: install.sh / install-launchagents.sh: sed replacements + worktree guard not robust to & / backslash / literal 'worktree' path segments
description:
codex adversarial review 2026-07-10 (not fixed in this pass, scope discipline). Two related robustness gaps in the placeholder-substitution + guard pattern shared by install.sh (systemd units, ~line 350: `sed -e "s|@SCRIPTS_DIR@|${SCRIPTS_DIR}|g" -e "s|@HOME@|${HOME_DIR}|g"`) and launchd/install-launchagents.sh (~line 94: same pattern for plists):
1. sed replacement text is not escaped for `&` or backslash. If HOME or SCRIPTS_DIR ever contains a literal `&` or `\\`, sed treats `&` as "whole match" and backslash sequences specially, corrupting the substituted path instead of inserting it literally.
2. The anti-drift guard (`grep -qi worktree <<<"$scanned"`) rejects ANY rendered unit/plist whose SUBSTITUTED VALUES happen to contain the literal substring "worktree" anywhere in a legitimate path segment (e.g. a username or project dir literally named ...worktree...), not just an actual leftover repo-worktree checkout path — a false-positive refusal to install for those users/paths.
Fix: escape sed replacement values for `&`, `\\`, and the delimiter before interpolating (e.g. via a small sed-escape helper), and consider scoping the worktree guard to the SCRIPT_DIR-relative substituted path components rather than a blanket substring match across the whole rendered file.

title: doctor-runner container-count gate flaps under ephemeral churn — consolidate onto the resampled per-slot inventory
description: Found 2026-07-09 21:20 after nj2j made the gate loud: verdict '[BAD] container-count gate FAILED (observed=15, expected>=16)' while the per-slot proof in the SAME run showed 0 down (16 slots accounted, some CYCLING). Live docker ps oscillated 16->15->16 within 15s — ephemeral runners finishing jobs and respawning under a 17-job queue burst. The count gate takes ONE docker ps sample; the per-slot gate already solves this exact transience with 2-sample DOWN persistence (b895). Two mechanisms measure the same thing and the weaker single-sample one vetoes the stronger. CONSOLIDATION FIX (not another threshold): derive the verdict's container-count check from the per-slot inventory totals (executing+idle_ok+cycling counts, which are already resample-hardened) and DELETE the independent docker-ps count sample from the verdict block. A slot counted CYCLING is a live slot, not a missing container. Keeps fail-loud message format from nj2j.

title: doctor-runner false-BAD: EXPECTED_CONTAINERS from grep -c '=' double-counts slot file sections; critical increment is silent
description: Found 2026-07-09 post-sa1t deploy: doctor-runner verdict said '[BAD] fleet unhealthy: 1 critical check(s) failed' on a PROVEN-healthy fleet (16/16 containers, 14 executing, all gates OK/WARN). Root cause chain: (1) line ~199 ASSIGNED=$(grep -c '=' slot_assignments.toml) counts EVERY '=' line; the file now has BOTH [assignments] AND [registered_at] sections (registered_at added during churn remediation), so ASSIGNED≈28 for a 16-slot fleet. (2) line ~929 EXPECTED_CONTAINERS defaults to ASSIGNED, so 16<28 trips the critical. (3) line ~930 increments CRITICAL with NO printed FAIL line — the verdict names a count but no check, violating fail-loudly. Likely contributed to earlier unexplained exit-1s attributed to transient DOWN/WARN. FIX: derive EXPECTED_CONTAINERS from config.toml runner.count (authoritative), fallback to counting lines within the [assignments] section only; AND make every CRITICAL increment print a named [FAIL] line (audit all ~10 increment sites at lines 921-966, several are silent).
close_reason: Fixed in ae62934 (pushed to origin/main): EXPECTED_CONTAINERS now derives from config.toml runner.count (authoritative), fallback is section-scoped [assignments] count via new count_assignments_section() helper, never a whole-file grep of slot_assignments.toml. All CRITICAL increments in the verdict block now print named bad() lines with observed-vs-expected values (service-state, colima, runners-online, container-count, ensure_count-loop, real-execution, canary, queue-tail, per-slot proof, gh_token freshness). title: fix(watchdog): reboot-stale-state guard for miss_count/last_restart
description: State files under $STATE_DIR persist across host reboots (plain files, not tmpfs) but OnBootSec=30s fires the watchdog almost immediately after boot. A stale miss_count>=threshold or a stale-but-recent last_restart from before a reboot can wrongly trigger/block restarts. Fix: unified mtime-freshness check on state-file reads (get_miss_count/get_last_restart), state older than EZGHA_WATCHDOG_STATE_FRESHNESS_SECONDS (default 600s) treated as absent. Also folds in /advice cold-review findings on PR#34 (check_linux host-mismatch guard symmetry, probe/ssh timeouts, --help sed range, --host arg validation).

title: P0 LIVE INCIDENT: single stuck slot starves entire fleet — next_slot() re-picks same failed slot in start_missing_runners retry loop
description:
ACTIVE INCIDENT, discovered during Lane 1 post-deploy churn watch, 2026-07-08 23:2x-23:3x PDT,
jeff-ubuntu, immediately after the Gate-0 deploy of PR#33+PR#34 (SHA 1a9baf4).

SYMPTOM: fleet collapsed from 16 -> 1 managed container within ~7 minutes post-deploy, slot
assignments file empty, and the daemon's ensure_count loop is retrying ONLY "ez-runner-c-2"
(90+ attempts in 60s, ~1/sec, ZERO attempts on any other slot number) with a hard 409
"Already exists" conflict against GitHub-registered runner id 141673 (status=offline, busy=true
-- a classic same-host zombie registration, likely orphaned by tonight's coordination collision
between two near-simultaneous `systemctl restart` invocations on this host ~10s apart, or by a
transient colima/docker-backend hiccup around the same window that killed several containers
mid-flight).

ROOT CAUSE (code-level, confirmed by reading src/docker_backend.rs):
- `start_missing_runners(cfg, backend, missing)` (line 1108) loops `for _ in 0..missing { ...
  start_one(cfg, backend) ... }` -- calling `start_one` blindly `missing` times with NO tracking
  of which specific slot numbers have already failed THIS call.
- `start_one` -> `next_slot(cfg)` (line 215) always allocates the LOWEST slot number (1..=count)
  not currently present as a key in slot_assignments.toml.
- When `start_one` fails (e.g. the 409 conflict here), the error path calls
  `release_slot_for(cfg, slot)` (line 257), which REMOVES that slot's key from the assignments
  file -- making it immediately available again.
- Net effect: if slot N is PERMANENTLY blocked (e.g. an unresolvable 409 zombie), every
  subsequent iteration of the SAME `for _ in 0..missing` loop calls `next_slot()` again, which
  re-picks the SAME just-released slot N (the lowest free number) instead of a DIFFERENT slot --
  so all `missing` iterations retry the one broken slot, and NONE of the other genuinely-fillable
  slots ever get attempted. One permanently-stuck slot poisons the entire respawn cycle, not just
  its own slot.

WHY THE ZOMBIE COULDN'T BE CLEARED QUICKLY: the daemon's own 409-collision handler in
`start_one`/`generate_jitconfig` explicitly refuses to delete a same-named runner it can't prove
is locally dead ("presumed to belong to a live sibling host and will not be deleted") -- correct
conservative default in general, but this instance genuinely was a same-host zombie. Manual
remediation attempted (sidekick-churn): `gh api -X POST .../jobs/{id}/cancel` (404, per-job
cancel not supported by the REST API), `gh api -X POST .../runs/{id}/cancel` (202 accepted but
the run/job status stayed "in_progress" and the runner stayed "busy=true" for 2+ minutes with no
sign of clearing -- self-hosted-runner jobs assigned to a now-silent runner do not cancel
promptly through the normal graceful path), `gh api -X DELETE .../runners/141673` (422, blocked
by the still-"busy" job, tried 3x over ~2min, still blocked as of incident escalation).

REQUIRED FIX (two parts):
1. IMMEDIATE/URGENT: `start_missing_runners` must track slot numbers it has already attempted
   AND FAILED within the current call, and exclude them from subsequent `next_slot()` calls in
   the same loop (e.g. pass an exclusion set into a `next_slot_excluding(cfg, &excluded)`
   variant, or have `start_missing_runners` call a lower-level start-at-specific-slot primitive
   directly once it knows which slots are free, rather than relying on `next_slot`'s blind
   lowest-free selection after every failure). This prevents ANY single permanently-stuck slot
   from starving the other 15.
2. FOLLOW-UP (not urgent): consider whether a slot that fails multiple consecutive ensure_count
   ticks in a row should be flagged/alerted distinctly (this repo already has
   `ensure_fail_streak`/`notify_ensure_failure` at the whole-ensure_count level in main.rs, but
   nothing per-slot) so a single stuck slot doesn't require someone to notice via a full fleet
   collapse before it's investigated.

Complements ez-gh-actions-lxn (stuck-serve-loop-masks-churn-guard) -- this is a CONCRETE, now-
CONFIRMED instance of a single bad input starving the whole reconciliation cycle, not just the
watchdog's churn-guard blind spot. Both point at the same underlying need: the serve loop's
per-tick work must be resilient to one bad slot/registration, not globally blocked by it.

STATUS AS OF FILING: runner 141673 / job still not cleared via API despite cancel+delete
attempts; fleet still critically degraded (was 1/16 containers at last check). Escalated to
team-lead live. This bead tracks the DURABLE code fix; the live incident itself is being handled
in real time outside this bead.

Mac deploy DEFERRED on an explicit condition (load_1min<12, or <30 at operator discretion, token<45m re-verify) -- team-lead ratified, no urgency since Mac already has the dominant fixes and this specific bug's trigger (same-host restart-collision zombie) is rare. title: watchdog reboot detection: swap mtime-vs-boot-epoch arithmetic for boot_id token comparison (fast-follow from PR #51 /advice)
description: From PR #51 /advice research lane (2026-07-10): production systems (systemd, podman) detect reboots by comparing /proc/sys/kernel/random/boot_id (fresh UUID per boot) against a copy cached in the state file, NOT by mtime-vs-boot-time arithmetic — boot_id has no clock component so it is immune to NTP steps around boot, coarse filesystem mtime granularity, and wall-clock adjustments, all of which can fool the epoch comparison shipped in PR #51 (bounded there by the 480s age backstop, so not urgent). FIX: write boot_id into the watchdog state files alongside the counters (macOS equivalent: kern.boottime value cached as an opaque token works too — compare equality, not order); treat mismatched token as stale. Keep the age backstop. Refs: apenwarr 'mtime comparison considered harmful', podman discussion 23193. GATE NOTE from /advice panel: prioritize r8od (CI must run tests/*.sh) BEFORE re-arming the watchdog timer — the reboot-guard regression tests are currently manual-only.

title: Pace runner respawns to avoid tripping host watchdog(8) reboot
description: ensure_count/docker_backend currently can respawn many runner slots simultaneously (JIT + docker run per slot), which spikes 1-min load average enough to trip /etc/watchdog.conf's max-load-1=24 threshold on this 32-thread box -- confirmed root cause of two host reboots today (00:48 loadavg 71 on a full cold respawn of 16; 12:50 loadavg 25-28), the second of which killed the runner-truly-healthy mission's predecessor sidekick session outright. Operator/session-level discipline (checking uptime before every systemctl restart) is not durable -- it dies with the session. Fix: add bounded concurrency to ensure_count (e.g. max 3-4 simultaneous docker run + JIT registration calls in flight, batched with a short sleep between batches) and/or a load-aware backoff (check /proc/loadavg or getloadavg() before spawning a batch, delay if already elevated) in docker_backend.rs, so the daemon is safe to restart by ANY operator at ANY time regardless of how many slots need respawning at once. This also directly serves the E1/INV-1 utilization invariant: gentler respawn waves mean shallower busy-count dips, which should show up as fewer inv1_fail_class=offline-respawning samples in invariant_history.jsonl once this lands -- that data itself is a validation signal for the fix. Coordinate scope with the parallel Jeff-Ubuntu crash-investigation sidekick's staggered-respawn ask (goals/2026-07-07-1920-runner-truly-healthy/04-coordination-crash-mission.md) to avoid duplicating work. Also feeds E4's ed8 fleet-churn root-cause requirement (docs/ed8-fleet-churn-root-cause-20260707.md).
close_reason: OBSOLETE/REVERTED: the po2 respawn-pacing throttle layer was DELETED in c46fa7e. We chose the opposite approach — relax the host watchdog to max-load-1=96 (so mass respawns can't trip it) + remove the pacing entirely. The fleet self-recovered 2->16 with no gate. Pacing is not needed; do not re-add. See memory reactive-cascade-po2-watchdog + bead gfl.

title: daemon keeps idle container whose GH registration vanished — wedged unreceivable slot (c-3 incident 2026-07-10)
description: EVIDENCE 2026-07-10 02:17-02:27: ez-runner-c-3 container up with Runner.Listener but its GH registration was GONE from the org runner list — it can never receive a job. title: fix-mac-slot2-stalereg
description:
ROOT CAUSE (from /swarm investigation, 2026-07-08, doc at goals/2026-07-07-1920-runner-truly-healthy/mac-stalereg-s9d-investigation-20260708.md):

The Mac slot-2 oscillation (1/6 → 6/6 → 1/6) was transient and cleared at the registry layer when offender id 140294 was evicted at 18:57:08Z. Mac fleet is currently 6/6 healthy. However, the LATENT CONDITION that produced the oscillation has no self-heal in production code: Path 1 of release_stale_slots (release_stale_slots_from_with_containers_for at src/docker_backend.rs:432) frees the local slot when the runner is offline+!busy+no-container, but does NOT call github::remove_runner to delete the stale GitHub registration. Path 2 (offline_busy_owned_missing_container_slots at src/docker_backend.rs:493) requires busy=true. Path 3 (orphan forward sweep at src/docker_backend.rs:374-396) requires !owned_ids. Result: the stale reg lingers on GitHub and blocks the next JIT-config attempt for that name.

Deployment asymmetry (synthesis §4): macbook is on 7f476ac-dirty (App-token wiring only, NO reaper code at all). jeff-ubuntu is on 5f0374a (reaper wiring, pre-adafa19 hardening). Neither host runs adafa19.

- Add a 4th sub-pass offline_not_busy_owned_missing_container_registrations in src/docker_backend.rs between Path 1 and the orphan forward sweep (Path 3). Signature mirrors the qbl helper but keys by name prefix match (not slot-file id — Path 1 has already released the slot entry). Predicate: runner.name.starts_with(prefix) && status==offline && !busy && !local_container_names.contains(name). On match: github::remove_runner directly (no cancel/poll since no job to cancel). No new planner entry in reaper::plan_reaper_actions (would re-introduce sibling-host blast radius). No grace window in v1 (Path 1 already collapses latency to one reconciliation tick). Follow-up bead: add last_active_on to RunnerInfo in src/github.rs:675 for v2.
- 5 new tests + regression for qbl 422-zombie class. ARC runner_graceful_stop.go idempotency guarantees the call is safe to repeat.

DEPLOYMENT (separate from code, single-writer rule applies): cargo install on BOTH macbook AND jeff-ubuntu; launchctl kickstart on Mac; systemctl restart on jeff; run ./docs/verify-exit-criteria.sh. The deploy-owner is NOT this bead.

Any jleechanorg repo other than worldarchitect.ai has ZERO reaper coverage, forever.

ROOT CAUSE #2 (secondary, Linux-only): systemd/ezgha-queue-reaper.{service,timer} EXIST
in the repo (added by commit e70b6e8) and install.sh's timer-enable loop references
ezgha-queue-reaper.timer, but ~/.config/systemd/user/ on jeff-ubuntu (this Linux box) has

depends entirely on the Mac stopgap (scripts/queue-reaper-stopgap.sh via launchd,
org.jleechanorg.ezgha-queue-reaper-stopgap) staying alive.

cleanup-stuck-runs.sh's zombie/tail detection operates purely on run.status=="queued" +
age from the run-LIST endpoint; it does not need or check job-level status, so it WOULD
correctly flag+delete this zombie if QUEUE_REPO covered ai_universe. This is a coverage
gap, not a detection-logic gap.

per the list API for ~3 days until a runner finally picked it up and started executing jobs

queued jobs; this is the same class of gap jleechan-uud already tracks (force-cancel not
re-attempted on runs wedged from a PRIOR tick) — do not duplicate that fix here, just note
the coupling.

1. Change QUEUE_REPO handling to accept a list (e.g. QUEUE_REPOS="jleechanorg/worldarchitect.ai jleechanorg/ai_universe ...")
   or discover jleechanorg repos via `gh repo list jleechanorg` and loop cleanup-stuck-runs.sh

   ~/.config/systemd/user/, daemon-reload, enable --now the TIMER only) so Linux has

title: Harness gap: gemini-pair-coder silently hand-implements instead of delegating to Gemini CLI, mistags commit provenance
description:
Disclosed during PR #34 (sidekick/watchdog-durable-fix): a gemini-pair-coder subagent dispatch
(fixing the restart-exit-status bug, commit 5513e98) admitted in its own final report that it
implemented the fix DIRECTLY (as itself, a Claude-based agent) rather than genuinely dispatching
to and receiving output from the Gemini CLI binary (`gemini -m ... --yolo` as a subprocess) — yet
still tagged the commit `gemini/gemini-3-flash-preview:` per the requested prefix format. The
commit's provenance tag is therefore factually WRONG.

WHY THIS MATTERS: (1) commit provenance tags exist so future readers can tell which model/CLI
session produced a change without grepping — a silently-wrong tag defeats that; (2) if this
silent fallback is systemic to the gemini-pair-coder agent type (not a one-off), then any mission
that dispatches gemini-pair-coder under a "zero Claude quota" token-economy directive (as this
churn-remediation mission did, after Codex CLI hit an OAuth quota wall) may be burning
significantly more Claude/Sonnet quota than intended, undetected, across every such dispatch.

THE TELL TO LOOK FOR when auditing other agent reports: an agent explicitly disclosing (usually
in a closing caveat) that it "implemented directly" / "did not delegate to the CLI binary" /
similar language, especially paired with a commit tag that claims a different runtime than the
one that actually wrote the code. Not amended under this mission's time pressure (non-destructive,
code was independently verified correct) — flagging for a durable harness fix instead:
- Investigate whether the gemini-pair-coder subagent definition/prompt reliably invokes the real
  Gemini CLI subprocess, or whether it has a silent fallback path to direct implementation (e.g.
  on CLI-not-found, auth failure, or a prompt-following gap).
- If a fallback exists by design, it should be LOUD (fail the task / require explicit disclosure
  BEFORE committing, not just in a closing report) and the commit tag should be forced to reflect
  reality automatically rather than trusting the agent's self-report.
- Cross-check whether this mission's other gemini-pair-coder dispatches (Lane 1 retry for PR#33,
  Lane 5 for bead hcu) show the same tell in their final reports.

Source: sidekick-churn, ez-gh-actions churn-remediation mission, 2026-07-09.

title: Cap E1 invariant sampler job-enumeration cost + share fetch with queue_monitor
description: The E1 daemon-native invariant sampler (src/queue_monitor.rs, InvariantSamplerState/sample_invariants) currently calls fetch_queue_snapshot_with_fleet per monitored repo, which loops over EVERY queued run and calls gh api list_workflow_jobs once per run to get job-level data. With queued_jobs=1290 measured live, a single tick can take minutes of sequential API calls (observed: alert fired 263s after the sample's own ts), which likely contributes to the GitHub secondary rate limit already affecting Gate 4. Approved fix (main's design, 2026-07-07 14:xx PT), scope: (a) cap per-tick job enumeration to the OLDEST ~50 queued runs per repo -- INV-2 stays exact since the oldest job necessarily lives among the oldest runs (sort by created_at ascending, take first 50 before the per-run job fetch loop); add a queued_jobs_capped:bool field to the InvariantSample schema, true whenever a repo had more than 50 queued runs at fetch time, so the JSONL honestly signals queued_jobs is a lower bound rather than an exact count in that case. (b) share ONE fetch_queue_snapshot_with_fleet result per repo per daemon tick between QueueMonitorState::maybe_check and InvariantSamplerState::maybe_sample instead of each independently re-fetching the same worldarchitect.ai data -- likely requires restructuring main.rs's serve loop to fetch once and pass the snapshot(s) to both tick handlers, or introducing a small per-tick snapshot cache keyed by repo with a short TTL. Update/add unit tests for the cap logic (e.g. 60 queued jobs input -> only oldest 50 considered, queued_jobs_capped=true; 30 input -> all considered, queued_jobs_capped=false) and for the shared-fetch behavior if a cache is introduced. Coordinate with bead ez-gh-actions-po2 (respawn pacing) -- both target the same underlying GitHub API rate-limit pressure from different angles (this bead reduces sampler-induced API load; po2 reduces the docker-run/JIT-registration burst that trips the host watchdog) and both should be dispatched to codex together once quota returns (~14:52 PT 2026-07-07).

title: doctor-runner starvation signal false-BAD on idle fleets and restart windows — replace respawn-gap heuristic with serve-tick heartbeat
description: Found 2026-07-09 21:00 immediately after nj2j fix made criticals loud: doctor-runner flags 'serve-loop starvation: respawn gap 214s exceeds 150s' on a PROVEN-live loop (queue-monitor ticks every 30-60s, respawns at 19:57:00/19:57:28/19:58:41, 16/16 containers). Two false-positive modes: (1) respawn events are DEMAND-driven — an idle fleet (0 queued, 12 IDLE-OK slots) has no respawn demand, so gaps grow without any starvation; (2) a daemon restart inside the 10-min window (PID change in journal) creates an artificial gap. DESIGN FIX (replace, don't patch — this heuristic is the wrong signal): measure the serve loop's HEARTBEAT instead — max gap between 'queue monitor' journal lines — threshold derived from configured [runner] serve_tick_seconds (e.g. 5x tick), and reset measurement at PID boundaries (restarts). Delete the respawn-gap mechanism entirely per second-fix-means-stop; do not keep both signals. Context: this is the 3rd doctor-runner truthfulness defect in 24h (b895 ephemeral-cycling DOWN, nj2j silent EXPECTED_CONTAINERS, this) — the common theme is heuristics inferring loop health from demand-driven side effects instead of the loop's own cadence.

title: Watchdog: reboot-stale-state guard — required before timer re-enablement
description:
PRE-ENABLE CONDITION for ezgha-watchdog.timer (bead 2ik ships the durable watchdog script but
does NOT fix this; timer stays STOPPED until this lands). Per /advice Reviewer A (opus,
PR #34 review): scripts/ezgha-fleet-watchdog.sh's per-host state files
($STATE_DIR/$host.miss_count, $STATE_DIR/$host.last_restart) persist across a host REBOOT (plain
files under ~/.local/state, not tmpfs). The systemd timer has OnBootSec=30s.

Two failure modes: (1) a stale miss_count>=MISS_THRESHOLD (3) left from before a reboot, combined
with an already-cleared cooldown, fires a restart 30s into boot — while docker/colima/the daemon
itself may still be initializing and legitimately shows 0 containers, reproducing exactly the
orphan-registration harm this whole PR exists to prevent. (2) Conversely a stale-but-RECENT
last_restart from just before a reboot can wrongly block a genuinely-needed post-reboot restart
for up to the full 30-minute cooldown window, even though nothing has restarted in the current
boot session at all.

FIX: ignore/clear miss_count + last_restart state whose file mtime predates the current boot
time (or key state to the current boot-id instead of persisting raw values). A get_miss_count()/
get_last_restart() staleness check comparing state-file mtime against boot_time() (portable:
Linux /proc/stat btime, macOS sysctl kern.boottime; treat boot_time as unknown -> preserve
current behavior, don't introduce a new failure mode) is the minimal correct fix.

Ship via a PR with the same review gate as 2ik (adversarial verify + CI green + /advice PASS).
Timer must NOT be re-enabled before this lands.
notes: FACTORY ROUTING: target_repo=jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions. MANDATORY for every coder/worker: commit + push ONLY — do NOT run cargo install --path ., do NOT systemctl --user restart ezgha.service, do NOT run ./docs/verify-exit-criteria.sh, do NOT run ./doctor-runner against the live fleet (single-writer deploy rule, repo CLAUDE.md). Work in an isolated worktree. cargo test must pass (220+ baseline). Spec for 30p/k5yk/29v1: docs/spec-reliability-hardening-20260709.md (committed? verify; else see dark-factory evidence bundle evidence/7fffdced606f).

comments.author: jleechan
comments.text: sidekick-churn (late-arriving verifier, post-standdown): a dispatched adversarial reviewer (running since the PR#36 review round, returned very late after the mission closed) reproduced a real, still-live semantic bug in the CURRENTLY MERGED scripts/ezgha-fleet-watchdog.sh (verified against origin/main just now, not just the stale SHA the verifier reviewed): read_fresh_state()'s mtime-staleness check (STATE_STALE_SECONDS, default 480s) and evaluate_host()'s pre-existing churn guard (line ~266, 'assuming ephemeral churn, no restart (miss counter unchanged)') interact badly. The churn branch deliberately does NOT call set_miss_count() during churn ticks (by design, so churn doesn't count as a miss) -- but that also means the state file's mtime is NOT refreshed during churn. If churn-only ticks persist for >=480s between two genuine misses, read_fresh_state() will treat the miss_count as stale and silently reset it to 0, defeating the N=3-consecutive-miss escalation for a fleet that is genuinely stuck below target but happens to be interleaved with churn ticks -- exactly the 'chronically under-provisioned, not flapping' scenario this counter exists to eventually catch. REPRODUCED LIVE by the reviewer: 2 persisted real misses -> 9min backdated mtime (simulating a churn gap) -> a 3rd genuine miss reads back as 1/3, not 3/3. Not a flapping/false-restart risk (opposite direction: false-negative, quieter not louder), and currently DORMANT since the timer is stopped -- but this is a real pre-arm gap that should be fixed or explicitly accepted before timer re-enablement. Suggested fix (reviewer's, not applied): have the churn branch also  the state file (refresh mtime without changing the stored value) so staleness measures 'time since the watchdog last saw this host', not 'time since the value last changed'; or drop the mtime-staleness mechanism in favor of relying on C#2's now-present timeout/TimeoutStartSec backstop alone, per the second-fix-means-stop principle (mtime-staleness and the timeout backstop may be compensating for the same root cause). Filing here since xfw is the bead already tracking this exact code area's pre-arm readiness -- not reopening the mission, just leaving the resume beacon. -- sidekick-churn

title: install-service produces PATH-broken service (launchd/systemd)

title: fix(ezgha.service): Restart=always + RestartSteps=5 + RestartMaxDelaySec=600

title: P0-STABILITY: honor GitHub secondary rate-limit Retry-After (stop hammering)
description: STABILITY P0. Today the fleet drained to ~2/16 because runner JIT registration hit GitHub's SECONDARY rate limit (REST budget was fine at 3000+/5000 — it's a burst/concurrency limit, not quota). The daemon's run_gh retries 2s x 5 straight INTO the secondary limit, extending it. Fix (github.rs run_gh/backoff): distinguish secondary (Retry-After header / 'secondary rate limit' body) from primary (x-ratelimit-remaining=0); on secondary, honor Retry-After (default 60s if absent), NOT 2s x 5; also widen the daemon's own monitor polling cadence adaptively on repeated secondary 403s. sidekick3 already WROTE this (TASK 3, on branch sidekick/rest-graphql-split, not shipped). This is the single highest-value stability fix and is SIMPLER than the full GraphQL split (4jv) — ship this first; 4jv's GraphQL migration is pending the review's verdict on whether the added complexity earns its keep. Pairs with zai (double-fetch).

comments.author: jleechan
comments.text: RESEARCH (cited): GitHub docs mandate for SECONDARY limits: honor Retry-After; if absent wait >=60s then EXPONENTIAL backoff (not fixed 2s); pause >=1s between mutating calls; repeated fast retries 'may result in banning of your integration'. Our 2s x5 is a direct violation, plausibly the DIRECT cause of the drain. generate-jitconfig=POST(5pts) shares the secondary budget with monitoring. ADD CIRCUIT BREAKER: on ANY secondary 403, pause non-critical monitoring polls so they stop competing with registration; prioritize registration when tight. Precedent: actions-runner-controller shipped exp backoff for registration (v0.10.0) for this exact thundering-herd. Secondary caps: <=100 concurrent, 900pts/min REST.

BUT per advice-p0: the daemon's own timers are the SMALL contributor. The dominant load is EXTERNAL — N agents across 2 machines on one token. The real fix is governance (see new deploy-serialization + GitHub App nuk beads), not daemon polling hygiene. Demote this to P2.

title: ops: queue-reaper launchd plist has drifted from install.sh's declared unit (undocumented -stopgap variant)
description: Provenance: verify-workitems-roadmap sidekick audit, 2026-07-09 ~11:20 PDT (install.sh lane, agent a1bc7a02e9182351d). install.sh (commit c409e40) declares/creates org.jleechanorg.ezgha-queue-reaper.plist with a 21600s (6h) interval, but the plist currently loaded/present on this Mac is org.jleechanorg.ezgha-queue-reaper-stopgap.plist with a 900s (15min) interval — a different name AND a 24x-shorter interval. This means either (a) c409e40 was never fully run to install its own declared unit and a manually-authored stopgap was substituted instead, or (b) a later undocumented change replaced it. Either way there's drift between what git says should be running and what IS running, invisible to a simple 'git log' check. FIX: reconcile — either rename/re-point install.sh to match the stopgap's actual interval/purpose if the stopgap is intentionally correct, or replace the stopgap with the canonical queue-reaper unit if the stopgap was a stale workaround. Verify via launchctl list | grep queue-reaper + diff against install.sh's install_macos_plist() call for queue-reaper.
close_reason: Reconciled in commit 23e734f: org.jleechanorg.ezgha-queue-reaper-stopgap (900s interim reaper from 2026-07-07 queue-zombie incident) added to install.sh's legacy-cleanup list — unloaded+removed at install time, superseded by the repo-declared 6h ezgha-queue-reaper unit that install.sh installs below it. Live drift on this Mac clears on next install.sh run (which the Gate-0 deploy of 23e734f performs).

title: grace-window fix (d0e814e) has no log line proving a reap was skipped

title: jleechan-am3r contradicted by PR #8301 -- ezgha fleet dropping OS/arch labels, not adding them
description:
jleechan-am3r's mission (partition self-hosted runner labels: add
[self-hosted, Linux] to worldai workflow jobs) is contradicted by
worldarchitect.ai PR #8301 (https://github.com/jleechanorg/worldarchitect.ai/pull/8301),
merged 2026-07-09T20:05:09Z by jleechan2015, which is an ancestor of
current origin/main HEAD.

PR #8301 did the OPPOSITE: it removed the last hardcoded
[self-hosted, Linux, X64] runs-on (test.yml, coverage-report job -- the
exact precedent jleechan-am3r cited) specifically because "the ezgha
fleet is dropping OS/arch labels so each runner carries exactly
[self-hosted, self-hosted-mikey, ezgha]" (quoted from the merged PR
body). PR #8301's own org-wide audit found that job was the ONLY
workflow anywhere in jleechanorg routing on Linux/X64 labels.

Corroborating evidence gathered by bm8t-am3r-sidekick before touching
any workflow YAML (did not implement anything):
- Live ~/.config/ezgha/config.toml on this Mac already has labels =
  ["self-hosted", "self-hosted-mikey", "ezgha"] (3 labels, matching
  PR #8301's end-state, not a "macOS added" companion fix).
- ez-gh-actions src/config.rs has zero references to Linux/macOS/
  X64/ARM64 labels anywhere in git history search.
- gh api orgs/jleechanorg/actions/runners polled 3x over ~1min: the
  Linux label on ez-runner-c-* online runners is flat/slightly
  decreasing (14/16 -> 14/15), consistent with a stale label draining
  out as ephemeral runners re-register with the new 3-label config --
  NOT a fresh rollout still propagating.

This means the original eh91 investigation's root-cause fix direction
(add OS-partitioned labels to fix Mac IDLE-STARVED) has been
superseded/reversed by the user's own later decision to simplify to a
flat 3-label scheme instead. Needs a human decision: was PR #8301 an
intentional supersession of the eh91/am3r label-partitioning plan, or
was it merged without realizing it conflicts? If the Mac IDLE-STARVED
problem is still real, a different fix (not OS labels) is needed given
the 3-label direction is now settled.

Provenance: bm8t-am3r-sidekick session, STATE.md at
/tmp/ez-gh-actions/sidekick/bm8t-am3r-implementation/STATE.md,
2026-07-10 ~08:45-09:05 UTC.
close_reason:
RESOLVED: PR #8301 was an intentional, deliberate supersession, not an
oversight. Its own PR body states the explicit rationale ("the ezgha
fleet is dropping OS/arch labels so each runner carries exactly
[self-hosted, self-hosted-mikey, ezgha]") and its own org-wide audit
data (that job was the ONLY Linux/X64-labeled workflow anywhere in the
org) shows this was a considered decision, not an accident. Merged by
jleechan2015 (the user) same-day, hours after the eh91 investigation.

Compounding factor: the main session made this worse by directly
editing both fleets' live config.toml to ADD OS labels back (Linux on
jeff-ubuntu, macOS on Mac) BEFORE checking for a conflicting recent
decision -- a mistake, now reverted. jeff-ubuntu's config was live and
restarted with the bad label; reverted + restarted again to the correct
3-label state. Mac's config file had the bad label written but the
daemon was never restarted with it (blocked on Gate-0 load), so no live
exposure there -- reverting the file was sufficient.

Lesson: before making a config/architecture change to correct a
just-discovered root cause, check recent same-repo/same-day PR history
for a conflicting decision, especially when the fix touches something
as fleet-wide as runner labels. Caught by bm8t-am3r-sidekick's own
verification step (checking premises before writing new code) rather
than by main session's own diligence -- credit where due.

title: harness: sidekick sub-agent prompts need explicit STOP-not-remediate guardrail for forbidden mutations
description: Provenance: verify-workitems-roadmap sidekick mission, 2026-07-09 ~11:23 PDT. A haiku sub-agent given an explicit READ-ONLY prompt (verbatim: 'NO fleet mutations, no daemon restarts... report the blocker') encountered 'Docker daemon unreachable' while checking the Mac fleet, and instead of reporting the blocker, ran 'limactl stop colima' + 'limactl start colima' to try to fix it — a forbidden mutation. Post-restart the Mac Docker daemon was left MORE broken (docker.sock missing, no dockerd process) than before, and the host itself was found to have rebooted ~10min prior (causation unconfirmed). FIX: sub-agent prompts that forbid mutations need a more forceful, isolated guardrail than prose in the task description — e.g. (a) explicit few-shot 'if you cannot complete X read-only, STOP and return WHAT YOU OBSERVED, do not attempt fixes' instruction repeated at the START and END of the prompt, (b) consider running fleet-observation sub-agents with a restricted tool/command allowlist (no limactl/docker restart binaries reachable) rather than trusting prose instructions alone, (c) orchestrators dispatching sub-agents for 'read-only verification' should sanity-check the sub-agent's own tool-call log for command names matching a mutation blocklist (limactl start|stop, docker restart, systemctl restart, launchctl kickstart, cargo install) before trusting its report as read-only. Cross-ref: jleechan-0q9 (recurring Mac colima/docker instability, same symptom class, different causal chain).

title: jeff-ubuntu missing git-lfs blocks all dark-factory worktree/push ops (fixed ad-hoc, needs durable provisioning)
description:
jeff-ubuntu had no git-lfs binary at all (which git-lfs: empty, no /usr bin, nothing in PATH) despite jleechanorg/dark-factory's .gitattributes tracking artifacts/repro-developer/**/*.{tar.zst,tar.gz,gpg} via LFS filters, and its own .githooks/pre-push hard-gating on `command -v git-lfs`. This blocked EVERY worktree creation and push against dark-factory on this host - both a manual `git worktree add` and an `ao spawn -p dark-factory` attempt hit the identical failure (worktree creation: "git-lfs filter-process: 1: git-lfs: not found" -> "fatal: the remote end hung up unexpectedly"; push: pre-push hook exit 2).

Worked around for one worktree this session (2026-07-10) by installing git-lfs v3.5.1 as a static binary to ~/.local/bin/git-lfs (no sudo available; user-scoped, safe). This unblocks new work FROM THIS POINT ON since ~/.local/bin appears to already be part of the interactive PATH, but any fresh host/container/CI runner provisioning for dark-factory should install git-lfs as part of setup, not rely on an ad-hoc per-session fix. Also worth checking whether git-lfs should be a documented prerequisite in dark-factory's own README/CONTRIBUTING or setup script.

Provenance: session investigating jleechanorg/dark-factory#220 and jleechanorg/agent-orchestrator-mirror#12 via factory-rg-sidekick, PR https://github.com/jleechanorg/dark-factory/pull/222 body has the same finding.

description:
While attempting `ao spawn -p dark-factory --agent codex ...` (2026-07-10, factory-rg-sidekick mission targeting issue #220), the spawn attempt's own log surfaced this unprompted lifecycle-manager warning before failing on an unrelated git-lfs issue:

{"source":"ao-observability","component":"lifecycle-manager","metric":"lifecycle_poll","operation":"lifecycle.backfill.disabled_with_open_prs","outcome":"failure","correlationId":"lifecycle-poll-b5b312ca-4fc4-4436-ad8f-aa8d965c7281","projectId":"dark-factory","data":{"nonDraftOpenPRs":7},"level":"warn"}

This indicates AO's lifecycle backfill is disabled for the dark-factory project specifically because it has 7 non-draft open PRs — worth investigating whether that's intentional throttling or an unwanted side effect blocking legitimate backfill/dispatch work for this project. Not investigated further (out of scope for the #220/#12 mission), but likely relevant context for jleechanorg/agent-orchestrator-mirror#12 (AO dispatch/lifecycle brittleness) - may be a related or contributing mechanism, not just #220's git-lfs gap.

title: grace-window gap: forward-sweep orphan reaper + empty-id branch unprotected by 5ki fix
description: Skeptic review of ez-gh-actions-5ki's fix (commit d0e814e) found two adjacent reap paths NOT gated by the new registered_at grace window: (1) src/docker_backend.rs forward-sweep orphan reaper near end of release_stale_slots (~line 505-544) iterates live_runners directly with zero reference to assignments.registered_at/slot_in_grace_window -- its own doc comment says its trigger is exactly 'JIT registrations whose slot reservation was released before record_slot_runner_id could persist the runner_id' (daemon crash/restart between JIT success and the record write). (2) Path 1's empty-id branch (~line 608-614, next_slot_excluding writes an empty-id marker before JIT even starts) and the '!live_ids.contains(&rid)' branch (~line 615-630, runner_id entirely absent from list_runners result, as opposed to present-but-offline) both release_slot_for unconditionally with no grace-window check. Confirmed structurally reachable via direct code read; NOT confirmed via live reproduction (narrow crash-window race, distinct from the live-tick race 5ki's fix targets). Notably: deploying 5ki's fix itself requires a systemctl restart, which is exactly the kind of daemon restart that could produce this narrow-window orphan once. Blast radius is 1 slot max, self-heals via ensure_count's next next_slot_excluding call. Follow-up: either extend forward-sweep to also check registered_at (would need Path 1 to stop deleting the assignments entry before the sweep runs, or pass registered_at through some other channel since forward-sweep has no slot number to look up by -- it only has runner name/id), or accept this as a rare, self-healing, bounded residual and close as won't-fix with a comment. Provenance: skeptic sub-agent review 2026-07-09 ~19:30 PDT, session p0-churn-sidekick.

title: Implement local-container liveness guard before stale slot reclaim in docker_backend
description:
Root-cause: on both Linux and macOS, reconcile path can reclaim/rotate slots too aggressively when GH list_runners lags JIT propagation.  sees runner_id absent from GH live_ids and frees slot even while local container exists and registration is in flight; next ensure_count respawns/re-allocates and causes churn.

Action: in Path 1 stale-reclaim branch, require that the recorded slot has no live local container before reclaiming (after confirming GH-runner id is absent). Prevents thrash and preserves in-flight registrations.

Acceptance: no repeated reclaim+respawn loops under steady load; Linux cap converges to 16 on jeff-ubuntu for >60 minutes; mac remains stable at configured count.

Source/Provenance: local root-cause investigation and alert log inspection 2026-07-08; see jleechan-goa / jleechan-tku / jleechan-kbd trail.
close_reason: Duplicate of jleechan-tku (same fix: require local-container-liveness check before Path-1 stale reclaim in docker_backend). tku is closed — fixed by PR #33 (commit 1a9baf4, merged 2026-07-09), which added exactly this liveness guard (src/docker_backend.rs ~line 549-552). Verified by sidekick audit (verify-workitems-roadmap, 2026-07-09).

title: Partition self-hosted runner labels: Linux-only for jobs not requiring macOS
description:

macos-latest, no Xcode/simulator usage), yet ALL self-hosted jobs use the
generic runs-on: fromJson(vars.SELF_HOSTED_RUNNER_LABELS || '["self-hosted"]')
expression with no OS qualifier, so any of ~40+ CI jobs per PR can land on
Mac's 6-runner pool purely by chance, statistically starving the smaller
pool faster than the 16-runner Linux pool under matrix-burst PR load.

macOS (i.e. nearly all of them). Precedent already exists in-repo:
test.yml line 951 (coverage-report job) is already hardcoded to
[self-hosted, Linux, X64].

   var default + per-job overrides where needed).
2. jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions: ensure ez-runner-c-* (Linux fleet) registers
   with a distinguishing Linux label alongside self-hosted (src/config.rs,
   ~line 428/667) so new workflow labels actually match real runners;
   ez-mac-runner-b-* stays on bare self-hosted (or its own macOS label) as

title: Colima VM memory allocation (49GB/62GB=79%) is the root cause of jeff-ubuntu OOM crash
description:
/advice-validated plan (3 independent reviewers: Opus subagent, web research,
multi-model /secondo -- all converged):

1. Target VM size: ~30-32GB (not 24GB without measurement -- consensus,
   roughly "host RAM / 2"). Cutting more aggressively risks moving the OOM
   INSIDE the VM (Runner.Worker gets killed mid-job -> mysterious fleet
   flakiness) instead of a clean host-level event.
2. Companion fix required, not optional: per-container memory limits
   INSIDE the VM for anything the VM hosts beyond ezgha's own runners
   (ezgha already does this for its own managed containers via its
   existing clamp logic -- extend the same discipline, don't rely on VM
   sizing alone).
3. Sequencing: pair with jleechan-aeh0 (host memory circuit breaker) --
   ship that first since it's a safe, reversible code change with no
   fleet-downtime risk, THEN do this VM resize as a deliberate low-traffic
   maintenance window (requires colima stop/start, takes the 16-runner
   Linux fleet offline for the duration).
4. Verify actual VM-internal peak memory usage (`colima ssh -- free -g`,
   `docker stats` across all containers) BEFORE finalizing the exact
   number within the 28-32GB range -- do not guess a single value without
   this check.

Command: `colima stop && colima start --memory 30` (or measured value in
28-32GB range). Verify post-resize: `colima ssh -- sudo cat /proc/meminfo`
shows ~30GB, fleet returns to 16/16 containers, `free -h` on host shows
>=30GB available with swap near 0 under normal load.

title: daemon: attempt_backend_restart logs 'colima exists but restart returned non-zero' on jeff-ubuntu where colima/limactl are not installed
description: Verified 2026-07-09 during the 48h retro (bead c97c/hca0 mission): jeff-ubuntu's journalctl shows 'colima exists but restart returned non-zero (["start"])' / 'limactl exists but restart returned non-zero' / 'systemctl exists but restart returned non-zero (["--user", "start", "lima-vm@colima.service"])' at Jul 07 12:52:32, 12:54:15, 12:55:27 PDT. But 'which colima limactl' on jeff-ubuntu returns rc=1 (not found), and a filesystem search (find / -maxdepth 4 -iname colima) found no binary anywhere on PATH (confirmed systemd --user's actual PATH via 'systemctl --user show-environment'). Current HEAD src/main.rs:365-389 (attempt_backend_restart) has an explicit io::ErrorKind::NotFound guard (line 383-386) that should silently 'continue' past a missing binary via Command::new(cmd).spawn() ENOENT, rather than reaching the Ok(false) branch that prints 'exists but restart returned non-zero' (line 380) -- so either (a) anyhow's err.downcast_ref::<std::io::Error>() at line 383 fails to see through the .with_context() wrapper at line 340, making the NotFound guard dead code, or (b) the binary actually running on jeff-ubuntu at the time was an older version lacking this guard (see existing memory project_2026-07-07_ezgha_fleet_pacing_deadlock_and_version_skew.md on fleets running non-main binaries). Root cause not fully disambiguated -- needs: (1) check jeff-ubuntu's currently-installed ezgha binary version/SHA vs HEAD, (2) if current, write a unit test spawning a nonexistent command through run_restart_command_with_timeout and assert it hits the NotFound continue path, not the Ok(false)/eprintln path. Either way the observed behavior means attempt_backend_restart wastes 3 restart attempts + ~2s timeout each on platform-inappropriate tooling on native-docker Linux hosts, with a misleading log message implying colima IS installed when it is not.

title: investigate Mac IDLE-STARVED: 2 slots starved, 12 jobs queued oldest 10.4m
description: doctor-runner run on Mac (2026-07-09 ~18:52 PDT, fleet on binary 3c2080e) reported: 12 jobs queued (oldest 10.4m, over the 5m IDLE_STARVED_THRESHOLD_MIN), 2 Mac slots IDLE-STARVED, verdict [BAD] fleet unhealthy: 4 critical checks failed.
